prompt: The venue owner signals you're approaching the noise curfew. You have two songs left.

answers.label: Respect the curfew — wrap up gracefully

outcomes.description: You finish on time. The owner says you're welcome back anytime.

answers.label: Play the last song anyway — beg forgiveness later

outcomes.description: The owner lets it slide. The crowd rewards you for going the extra mile.
The owner pulls the plug mid-song. You're not welcome back.
The owner agrees to five more minutes. You make them count.
The owner says no. You end on a slightly flat note.

prompt: Someone in the front row holds up a sign: 'It's my birthday — can you play my favourite song?' It's not on the setlist.

answers.label: Play the request — make their night

outcomes.description: The artist nails it from memory. The crowd sings along spontaneously.
A few fumbled chords, but the sentiment wins everyone over anyway.

answers.label: Acknowledge them but stay on setlist

outcomes.description: You wish them happy birthday over the mic. They smile and clap.

answers.label: Invite them on stage for a shoutout

outcomes.description: They're a great sport. The crowd absolutely loves the chaos.
They freeze on stage. Awkward three minutes for everyone.

prompt: The open mic act before you kills it and the crowd is hyped for them, not you. How do you respond?

answers.label: Feed off their energy and match the hype

outcomes.description: You channel the room's energy perfectly. The crowd transfers their excitement to you.
You come out swinging too hard and burn out early.

answers.label: Start slow and build — let the crowd come to you

outcomes.description: A measured opening wins them over gradually.
Too slow. The crowd never fully transitions over.
